DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS MARIO CANEL, MARCOS GONZALEZ MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR ISSUE OR EVIDENCE SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANT ROGER MAC'S ANSWER IS STRICKEN AND PLAINTIFF MAY ENTER DEFAULT WITH THE CLERK OF COURT. THE COURT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. FURTHER, THE PRIOR ORDER OF MARCH 16, 2011 GRANTING SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2240 IS VACATED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Mitch Lyon, #6848

Ashwin V. Ladva, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, MARCOS GONZALEZ. Plaintiff MARIO CANEL not present in court.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) FONG'S FORTUNE COOKIES, ROGER MAC, SHOU-MEI SMITH.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
Court ordered Defendants Does 1-25 dismissed without prejudice.
Marcos Gonzalez sworn and examinded on his own behalf.
Complaint filed by Plaintiff Mario Canel is dismissed without prejudice at the request of counsel for plaintiff. Counsel to file dismissal with the court.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff MARCOS GONZALES (only) and against Defaulted Defendant(s) FONG'S GORTUNE COOKIES, ROGER MAC, SHOU-MEI SMITH as follows:

Damages: $69,969.39
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0.00
Attorney's Fees: $8,360.00
Costs: $0.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $78,329.39
Judgment signed in open court.

Notice And Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or Issue Or Evidence Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS MARIO CANEL, MARCOS GONZALEZ MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR ISSUE OR EVIDENCE SANCTIONS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS.
Appearances: Roger Mac (510.717.2151) defendant and responding party appearing in propria persona; Daniel Martinez de la Vega of the Ladva Law Firm for plaintiffs and moving parties.
Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: granted, no opposition filed. Defendant Roger Mac's answer is stricken and plaintiff may enter default with the clerk of court. The court denied plaintiff's request for sanctions. Further, the prior order of March 16, 2011 granting sanctions in the amount of $2240 is vacated. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
